Geddy Lucier
Senior Thesis
Regression Pipeline

This document is designed to create a fluid data pipeline to the algonquin/chicago citygate regression comparison

Data Load-in preamble
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr) # Data Manipulation
library(lubridate) # Date manipulation
library(ggplot2) # Visualization
library(riem) # Weather api from airport observations


setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/Algonquin_NatGas//")

prices <- read_csv("gas_prices.csv") # comes from Bloomberg Terminal. 
```
Initial Price Cleaning 
```{r}
chicago <- prices[c(5,6)] %>% rename("Date" = "Date...5", "price" = "Last Price...6") %>%
      mutate(Date = as.Date(Date, format = "%m/%d/%Y"),
             city = "Chicago") 
             
          

algonquin <- prices[c(1,2)] %>% rename("Date" = "Date...1", "price" = "Last Price...2") %>%
     mutate(Date = as.Date(Date, format = "%m/%d/%Y"),
             city = "Boston") 

henry <- prices[c(3,4)] %>% rename("Date" = "Date...3", "Hen_Price" = "Last Price...4")

master_dates <- chicago %>%
  select("Date") %>%
  distinct()

start_date <- tail(master_dates$Date, n = 1)
end_date <-  head(master_dates$Date,1)

```

Weather Feature
```{r}
#Boston 
boston_weather <- riem_measures(
  station = "BED", # Bedford Station, not enough Boston Data
  date_start = start_date,
  date_end = end_date) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(valid)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%  # GROUP BY DATE FIRST
  summarise(
    temp_max = max(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_min = min(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_avg = mean(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    HDD = pmax(0, 65 - temp_avg),
    CDD = pmax(0, temp_avg - 65) ,
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

algonquin <- algonquin %>%
  left_join(boston_weather, by = c("Date" = "date"))


#Chicago 
chicago_weather <- riem_measures(
  station = "KORD",  
  date_start = start_date,
  date_end = end_date) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(valid)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%  # GROUP BY DATE FIRST
  summarise(
    temp_max = max(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_min = min(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_avg = mean(tmpf, na.rm = TRUE),
    
     
    HDD = pmax(0, 65 - temp_avg),
    CDD = pmax(0, temp_avg - 65) ,
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

chicago <- chicago %>%
  left_join(chicago_weather, by = c("Date" = "date"))

```
Regression Data 

```{r}

joint_df <- rbind(chicago, algonquin)

summary(lm(price ~ city + temp_min + temp_max + temp_avg, data = joint_df))

summary(lm(price ~ city + CDD + HDD, data = joint_df))

```
